BREAKING: Indian Army Leads Massive Rescue Operation in Gulmarg

Following a major technical fault that stranded dozens of gondola cabins mid-air in Gulmarg, troops of the Indian Army’s 9 Rajputana Rifles, alongside SDRF and J&K Police, launched a swift high-altitude rescue operation under challenging weather conditions.

Using ropes, casualty assistance teams, and all-terrain vehicles, rescue teams safely evacuated stranded tourists, including families, women, and children, with no injuries reported.

A powerful example of the Indian Army stepping in when civilians needed help the most. 🇮🇳

tweets.title:
Is this UP, India?

A Pakistani lady named Laila Khan released a video claiming she was roaming in UP, India. However, the video was actually shot in Nepal. This raises serious questions about why someone would falsely claim to be in India and what the intention behind such a video could be.

This concern is not limited to Laila Khan alone. Many Pakistanis are currently present in Nepal and Bangladesh. In Bangladesh, several Pakistanis have been seen making videos near the Indian border areas.

There is no issue with Pakistanis visiting friendly neighbouring countries. The concern begins when unwanted elements may try to misuse these locations to intrude into India with bad intentions.

RT by @OsintTV: "By the time you read this letter, I'll be watching over you from the skies..."

These unforgettable words were written by Captain Vijyant Thapar (Vir Chakra) shortly before he led his assault team to capture the strategic Three Pimples position during the Battle of Tololing in the 1999 Kargil War.

At just 22 years old, Captain Thapar laid down his life for the nation with unmatched courage and honor. Even in his final moments, his thoughts reflected humanity and selflessness — from expressing his wish to donate his organs to ensuring continued support for a young Kashmiri girl, Ruksana, whom he had met during deployment.
His bravery, compassion, and sacrifice continue to inspire generations of Indians.

"Live life king size" — a line that became immortal with him.
